name: Macroquad
description: Minimal, extremely fast-compiling cross-platform 2D/3D framework built on miniquad; excellent web support

kind.comment: Code-first game library with a render+input+audio loop and no editor; inspired by raylib.

license.comment: Dual-licensed MIT OR Apache-2.0 per crates.io metadata.

documentation.comment: API docs on docs.rs; the project also hosts tutorials/articles at macroquad.rs.

first-release.comment: First crates.io publish (0.2.7) in June 2020; repo created January 2020.

latest-stable-version.comment: Newest stable on crates.io as of 2026-07-06 (released 2026-05-20).

maintenance-status.comment: Last push 2026-06-15 and a 0.4.15 release 2026-05-20; ongoing commits from the lead maintainer.

maturity.comment: Still pre-1.0 (0.4.x) after ~6 years; API is stable in practice for 2D and used in shipped games, but occasional breaking changes between minor series.

backing.value: Community — solo lead maintainer (Fedor Logachev / @not-fl3), Patreon-supported
backing.comment: No company/foundation backing; primarily a single maintainer.

implementation.comment: Pure Rust on top of the pure-Rust miniquad backend; only thin platform glue (no engine *-sys bindings).

architecture.comment: Immediate-mode: draw-from-state game loop each frame; no scene graph or ECS.

built-in-ecs.comment: No ECS; game state is plain Rust structs in an immediate loop.

dimensionality.comment: 2D is the primary focus; first-party 3D exists (Camera3D, draw_cube/sphere/grid, meshes, first-person example) but is basic — no PBR, lighting, or material pipeline.

scripting.comment: Gameplay is written in Rust; no embedded scripting language.

data-driven-scenes.comment: No scene/prefab file format; scenes are constructed in code.

reflection-serialization.comment: No first-party runtime reflection or scene serialization; users bring serde themselves.

hot-reload.comment: No built-in code or asset hot-reloading in the core framework.

visual-editor.comment: Code-first framework; ships no graphical scene/level editor.

live-inspector.comment: No runtime world/entity inspector; the built-in immediate-mode UI can be used to hand-roll debug panels.

asset-pipeline.comment: Async loading of common formats (load_texture, load_sound, load_ttf_font) with ergonomic await; no import/processing pipeline or reprocessing.

cli-project-tooling.comment: No project-scaffolding CLI; a separate cargo-quad-apk cargo plugin exists for Android packaging but not for project setup.

debug-profiling-tools.comment: No first-party profiler/diagnostics overlay integrations in core.

ide-support.comment: Nothing beyond standard Rust tooling (rust-analyzer just works).

rendering-backend.comment: Renders through miniquad, which abstracts OpenGL/GLES on desktop, WebGL in browsers, and Metal on Apple platforms.

pbr.comment: No PBR pipeline; 3D support is limited to unlit primitives and meshes.

lighting.comment: No first-party lighting system (2D or 3D); users implement lighting via custom shaders.

shadows.comment: No built-in shadow mapping or 2D shadow support.

custom-shaders.comment: Custom GLSL shaders via the Material/shader API, slotted into the render pipeline.

sprites-tilemaps.comment: Sprite drawing with automatic geometry batching is built in; tilemaps come from the companion macroquad-tiled crate (Tiled maps), not core.

particle-system.comment: Not in core; a companion macroquad-particles crate by the same author provides a particle system.

post-processing.comment: No built-in post-processing stack; effects can be hand-rolled with render targets and custom shaders.

text-rendering.comment: Built-in text rendering: draw_text plus TTF font loading and a glyph atlas.

physics.comment: No physics in core; repo ships a hand-rolled platformer collision example, otherwise bring-your-own (e.g. rapier).

audio.comment: Built-in audio module (macroquad::audio) for loading and playing sounds.

input.comment: First-party keyboard, mouse, and touch input; native gamepad support is still work-in-progress per the docs ("gamepads soon").

animation.comment: No first-party animation system; sprite-frame animation is done manually by advancing source rects.

ui-system.comment: Ships a built-in immediate-mode UI library (macroquad::ui).

networking.comment: No built-in networking; the Fish Game demo used the external Nakama server.

gltf-model-import.comment: 3D is limited to primitives and simple meshes; no standard glTF/GLB importer in core.

macos.comment: Native macOS target officially supported (Metal via miniquad).

linux.comment: Native Linux target officially supported (requires alsa-lib for audio).

web-wasm.comment: First-class HTML5/WebAssembly target via WebGL; single-command WASM deployment is a headline feature.

ios.comment: iOS listed among officially supported platforms.

android.comment: Officially supported; single-command APK deployment (cargo-quad-apk). Zemeroth shipped to Google Play with macroquad.

consoles.comment: No documented, officially supported console path.

clean-build-time.comment: Not independently measured. The project README reports a clean build of ~16s on an old ThinkPad X230; fast compilation is macroquad's headline differentiator, but no verified measurement on a stated modern machine was made.

incremental-build-time.comment: Not measured this pass; inferred from macroquad's very small dependency tree (a one-line change mainly relinks the minimal binary). Estimate, not a benchmark.

binary-size.comment: Not measured this pass; minimal-dependency macroquad apps produce small stripped release binaries (a few MB). Estimate.

web-bundle-size.comment: Not measured this pass; minimal macroquad WASM bundles are typically a few MB. Estimate.

runtime-overhead.comment: Subjective: macroquad is a thin immediate-mode layer over miniquad with no ECS/scheduler, so idle overhead is very low. Not benchmarked.

crates-io-downloads.comment: All-time crates.io downloads of the macroquad crate as of 2026-07-06.

last-release.comment: Release date of the newest stable version 0.4.15.

shipped-titles.value: Zemeroth (turn-based tactics, on Google Play); Fish Game (online multiplayer demo)
shipped-titles.comment: Zemeroth is the most notable released macroquad game; Fish Game is a widely-cited multiplayer demo built with macroquad.

community-size.comment: Active but small: development is centred on the solo lead maintainer with a modest contributor base and Discord community.

plugin-asset-ecosystem.comment: A handful of companion crates (macroquad-tiled, macroquad-particles, egui-macroquad, etc.), mostly from the author; no large third-party plugin registry.

learning-resources.comment: Solid examples/ directory, docs.rs API docs, and tutorial articles on macroquad.rs; no full official book, and fewer videos/guides than Bevy.
